tagline: Yesterday's good news, bright and early.

stories.headline: From the Philippines to Wimbledon Glory: Alex Eala Stuns the World
stories.summary: Twenty-one-year-old Alex Eala from the Philippines defeats world No. 3 Iga Świątek at Wimbledon, becoming the first Filipino to reach the fourth round.

stories.headline: One Officer, One Theater, One Perfect Day for 144 Kids
stories.summary: Officer Sean Reavie rented an entire theater and bought snacks for 144 Arizona middle schoolers to watch Toy Story 5 together.

stories.headline: Messi's Embrace: A Moment of Pure Sportsmanship Between Giants
stories.summary: Cape Verde goalkeeper Vozinha receives an embrace from Lionel Messi, who tells him, 'You are great,' then swaps shirts with him after their World Cup match.

stories.headline: From Never Swimming to English Channel Champion: Ankita's Three-Year Dream
stories.summary: Assam's Ankita Konwar, who learned to swim just three years ago, becomes the first woman from Northeast India to complete an English Channel relay in 13 hours and 5 minutes.

stories.headline: Kids Fight Back—and Win: Michigan Unanimously Frees the Lemonade Stand
stories.summary: Brothers Ethan, Seth, and Jonathan Mielke's fight against a $57-per-week lemonade permit sparked a unanimous 110-0 Michigan legislative vote to free kids' stands.

stories.headline: Georgia's Bald Eagles Soar: From One Nest to 200 in Four Decades
stories.summary: Georgia's bald eagle population soared from virtually zero nests in the 1970s to 101 successful nests producing 170 fledglings this year—an 89% success rate.

stories.headline: A Stranger Returns a Grieving Family's Most Precious Treasure
stories.summary: A stranger who bought Rachel White's late father's treasured Australian hat for £10 at a charity shop returned it after seeing her Facebook plea.

stories.headline: British Wildcard Arthur Fery Refuses to Quit: A Four-Hour Wimbledon Thriller
stories.summary: Battling back from near-defeat in a grueling four-hour match, wildcard Arthur Fery reaches Wimbledon's fourth round in a breathless comeback.

stories.headline: Fifteen Years and Counting: Two Patients Live Drug-Free After Immune 'Reset'
stories.summary: Two 28-year-old patients remain relapse-free from severe autoimmune disease neuromyelitis optica for over 15 years after stem cell transplant, without ongoing medication.

stories.headline: Sweet Alice at 92: Still Giving Everything to Her Community
stories.summary: Ninety-two-year-old Sweet Alice distributed food, clothing, and essentials to Watts families, continuing her decades-long grassroots mission of care.

stories.headline: World's Tiniest Pigs Return Home: A Conservation Victory
stories.summary: Jersey's Durrell Wildlife Conservation Trust released 15 pygmy hogs—the world's tiniest pigs—into India's Manas National Park, part of a program that has restored 194 to the wild.

stories.headline: Horseshoe Crabs Return: Massachusetts Reaches Record Populations
stories.summary: After 25 years of protection, Massachusetts horseshoe crab populations hit record highs, with 90% of surveyed beaches showing strong growth.

stories.headline: Hope in a Nasal Spray: New TB Vaccine Offers Path for Drug-Resistant Cases
stories.summary: Johns Hopkins researchers developed an intranasal DNA vaccine that helps patients clear tuberculosis bacteria faster and prevents relapse after treatment ends.
